title: NOC 20011 – Architecture and Science Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Architecture and Science Managers lead major design, planning, and research operations across architectural firms, laboratories, and government agencies in Canada. Classified under NOC 20011, this TEER 0 management occupation continues to experience strong national demand—creating premium immigration opportunities through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.major_group: 200 – Specialized middle management occupations in engineering, architecture, science, and information systems
classification.minor_group: 2001 – Managers in engineering, architecture, science, and information systems

duties: Plan and oversee architectural or urban-planning services
Develop technical policies and professional standards
Consult with clients and present design proposals
Participate in major design and inspection projects
Direct laboratory research and production programs
Collaborate with regulators and industry partners
employment_requirements: Degree in architecture plus professional registration (for architectural managers)
Landscape-architecture degree and licensing (for landscape managers)
Master’s or doctoral degree in a scientific discipline (for science managers)
Several years of senior-level professional experience
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 18,700 new positions for Architecture and Science Managers, while about 15,200 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and executive-level immigration planning especially valuable for overseas professionals.
additional_information: Architecture and Science Managers remain in demand across urban planning, environmental research, industrial science, and advanced technology sectors nationwide. Hiring has expanded steadily since the pandemic and is expected to remain strong.
Architectural and urban-planning firmsScientific research laboratoriesMining, forestry, and manufacturing R&D divisionsGovernment science agenciesEnvironmental and agricultural research institutes
Infrastructure and sustainable-development projectsGrowth in clean-technology and environmental sciencesIndustrial research expansionSenior-level leadership demandEmployer-driven recruitment
Annual salaries typically range between $69,300 and $157,700 CAD, depending on province, discipline, and executive experience.
